attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. This is NET-NEW coverage (the 7th tranche-1 category, not a base-303 refresh) — ingest grows the eval corpus 303->353. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: the routine longitudinal management of a stable congenital/genetic/developmental disability is low-tier (self-care + specialist/allied-health/MDT referral + safety-net), while acute decompensations and time-critical complications are tiered to true acuity (T3-T5) with mandatory escalation edges — autonomic dysreflexia in spinal cord injury (T5/000), metabolic crises (MSUD/galactosaemia/mitochondrial/peroxisomal/hypoglycaemia), aspiration pneumonia, progressive neuromuscular respiratory failure, major burns, pressure-injury sepsis, status epilepticus, decompensated congenital heart disease, hypocalcaemic tetany, and demand myocardial ischaemia in supravalvular aortic stenosis. Paediatric no-dose hard limit honoured throughout (no synthesised mg/kg or weight-based dose; paediatric and unknown-weight cases flagged for in-person/specialist prescribing), no autonomous diagnosis, diagnostic-overshadowing encoded as the key error trap. Specific borderlines reviewed and accepted: (a) DCD-016 source file titled 'Perthes' but the clinical picture is developmental dysplasia of the hip in a 4-month-old — authored correctly as DDH, source title is a note-quirk only; (b) the T5-heavy tier profile (T3x9/T4x10/T5x31) is accepted as honest for a folder authored entirely as acute-complication presentations; (c) DCD-050 Williams syndrome panic attack tiered T5 is accepted (fixed supravalvular aortic stenosis + tachycardia -> demand myocardial ischaemia, the note's own primary differential is acute MI).
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DCD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-GENET-04-00232 — SEQ is provisional (derived from source DCD-032, placed in the 002xx band); maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-GENET-04 bucket at ingest --reseq.

Specialty: case_id carries the taxonomy code GENET (Medical Genetics & Genomics — the truest specialty for an inherited mitochondrial disorder), but that code is not in the 00 schema's specialty_tags enum, so case_metadata.specialty_tags uses the schema-valid ['EMG','ENDO','NEURO']. The id and the tags intentionally diverge per the id_rule (id is an opaque partition key; case_metadata is authoritative) — reviewer may re-tag.
Age (35) and sex (male) inferred from the note context ('spouse', 'his') — please confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who measured vitals (BP 95/55, HR 130, RR 32), performed a neurological/cardiovascular exam, gave oral glucose gel and activated 000 for IV dextrose/bicarbonate. Clinician-measured vitals and exam findings (Kussmaul respirations, ptosis/ophthalmoplegia, flaccid weakness, ragged red fibres) are sealed in 10/11; patient/carer-observable data (deep fast breathing, confusion, pallor, home glucose/SpO2/temperature/weight) are in 01.objective_data_offered, verified:false.

Home-obtainable objective data assumption: capillary glucose (4.0 mmol/L), SpO2 (97%) and temperature (37.2 C) are modelled as patient/home-device readings in 01; if the source intended these as clinician-measured, a reviewer should move them to the sealed side.

No doses synthesised: IV dextrose and IV sodium bicarbonate are recorded as the correct in-hospital pathway without dose (not specified numerically in the source); adult patient so no paediatric-dosing bar triggered, but ED/clinician sets all IV doses.
AMT/PBS drug codes not assigned (omitted) pending terminology receipt — condition SNOMED/ICD and observation LOINC candidates only; all codes marked unverified.

De-anchoring applied: the known lifelong mitochondrial disease is carried as patient-stated baseline history in 01; the NEW acute diagnosis (lactic-acidosis crisis with encephalopathy) and the T5/000 triage are sealed in 10-13.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
